flashcards.front: What does FIFO stand for, and what data structure uses it?
flashcards.back: First-In-First-Out. It is the fundamental principle of a Queue data structure.
The operation to add an element to the rear (or back) of a queue.
The operation to remove and return the element from the front of a queue.

flashcards.front: What is the main drawback of a simple linear queue implemented with an array?
flashcards.back: Memory wastage. After dequeues, empty spaces at the front of the array cannot be reused, leading to inefficient memory usage.

flashcards.front: Name one key application of queues.
flashcards.back: CPU Scheduling (e.g., the ready queue), Breadth-First Search (BFS), Print Spooling, or Buffering I/O devices.
